body:
Introduces the option to use `CO.OceanConfigurations.orca_ocean` and makes this the default configuration for weekly (CMIP) longruns. 

Closes https://github.com/CliMA/ClimaCoupler.jl/issues/1379
title: Add config option for ORCA grid

body: EN4 Monthly data only supports 5500m depth - this is set via kwarg in the tripolar grid config but was not done for the ORCA grid ; this PR fixes the max depth (set to 5500m, where the default 6km leads to `LoadError`).
title: Fix ORCA grid depth

body:
This PR adds a `Interfacer.progress` function that can extended by each component model to print some information on a callback interval. 

Changes:
- Config flag `ocean_progress_interval` is now a `String`, not an `Integer` (default: `"never"`)
- Config flag `atmos_progress_interval` added (default: `"never"`)
- Config flag `land_progress_interval` added (default: `"never"`)
- Config flag `seaice_progress_interval` added (default: `"never"`)
- `Interfacer.progress` function added with default warning, extended for `ClimaAtmos`, `Oceanananigans`, `ClimaLand`, and `ClimaSeaIce`
- `Intefacer.current_date` has a new method that allows for passing a component model's `t`
- Progress printing is turned on for all CI runs
- docs/tests updated accordingly
title: Add progress callbacks for all component models

body:
Temporarily disables the `main` branch tracking in the nightly coarse AMIP runs. 

See : https://github.com/CliMA/ClimaAtmos.jl/pull/4624 and https://github.com/CliMA/RRTMGP.jl/pull/601. 

SurfaceFluxes, likewise, is about to include a breaking change soon - the temporary changes in this PR attempt to keep nightly build running so that updates to ClimaAtmos.jl and ClimaTimesteppers can be tested. Upon updating to the new RRTMGP interfaces + verifying the new SurfaceFluxes solver changes, we can track main branches in both those packages.
title: Temporarily use latest compatible versions of SF / RRTMGP + Up deps
